title: OpenClaw strict inline-eval approval gate sequenced after the allowlist allow decision, letting shell positional carriers reach exec
description: In evaluateSystemRunPolicyPhase() (src/node-host/invoke-system-run.ts) the strict inline-eval approval gate is evaluated only after the generic policy decision: the code first returns on !policy.allowed and only then checks 'if (inlineEvalHit && !policy.approvedByAsk)'. When a command request combines allowlisted tools with shell positional arguments, inline-eval content is carried in a shell positional parameter that the allowlist evaluation treats as satisfied (policy.allowed === true). In that arrangement the strict inline-eval approval boundary does not fire as intended, so shell-provided inline-eval content can be executed outside the intended allowlist rule without the explicit approval strictInlineEval mode is supposed to require.
